about.summary: 'Rcpp' bindings for 'PLANC', a highly parallel and extensible NMF/NTF  (Non-negative Matrix/Tensor Factorization) library. Wraps algorithms described in Kannan et. al (2018) <doi:10.1109/TKDE.2017.2767592> and Eswar et. al (2021) <doi:10.1145/3432185>. Implements algorithms described in Welch et al. (2019) <doi:10.1016/j.cell.2019.05.006>, Gao et al. (2021) <doi:10.1038/s41587-021-00867-x>, and Kriebel & Welch (2022) <doi:10.1038/s41467-022-28431-4>.

raw_meta_yaml:
schema_version: 1

context:
  version: 1.0.0-rc3

package:
  name: r-rcppplanc
  version: ${{ version|replace("-", "_") }}

source:
  url: https://github.com/welch-lab/RcppPlanc/archive/refs/tags/${{ version }}.tar.gz
  sha256: 70fd068ed50acf99c07f1dc158f8884fe49a327eda796a5f95b6283cf719ae29
  patches:
    - patches/remove-hardcoded-dep-path.patch
    - if: not win
      then: patches/add-cxx-libc-availability.patch
    - if: win
      then: patches/Windows-explicit-options.patch
    - if: osx and arm64
      then: patches/osx-arm-find-r.patch
    - if: linux and build_platform != target_platform
      then: patches/linux-cc-r-link.patch

build:
  number: 1
  dynamic_linking:
    rpaths:
      - lib/R/lib/
      - lib/
    missing_dso_allowlist:
      - if: win
        then: "*/R.dll"
      - if: win
        then: "*/Rblas.dll"
      - if: win
        then: "*/Rlapack.dll"

requirements:
  build:
    - if: build_platform != target_platform
      then: cross-r-base ${{ r_base }}
    - if: build_platform != target_platform
      then: r-rcppprogress
    - if: build_platform != target_platform
      then: r-rcpp
    - if: build_platform != target_platform
      then: r-rcpparmadillo
    - if: build_platform != target_platform
      then: r-matrix
    - if: unix
      then: gnuconfig
    - if: not win
      then: ${{ compiler('c') }}
    - if: win
      then: ${{ compiler('m2w64_c') }}
    - if: not win
      then: ${{ compiler('cxx') }}
    - if: win
      then: ${{ compiler('m2w64_cxx') }}
    - if: win
      then: m2-filesystem
    - if: win
      then: m2-git
    - if: unix
      then: git
    - if: win
      then: m2-zip
    - if: not win
      then: ${{ stdlib("c") }}
    - if: win
      then: ${{ stdlib("m2w64_c") }}
    - cmake >=3.22
    - make
    - if: osx
      then: llvm-openmp
    - if: not osx
      then: libgomp
    - pkg-config
    - if: win
      then: m2-diffutils
    - if: unix
      then: diffutils
    - if: win
      then: m2-patch
    - if: unix
      then: patch
  host:
    - r-base
    - r-matrix
    - r-rcpp
    - r-rcpparmadillo
    - r-rcppprogress
    - libhwloc >=2.0.0,<3.0.0
    - zlib
    - hdf5 >=1.10
    - if: osx
      then: llvm-openmp
    - if: not osx
      then: libgomp
    - libblas
    - libcblas
    - liblapack
    - highfive <3
    - if: win
      then: dlfcn-win32
  run:
    - r-base
    - r-matrix
    - r-rcpp
    - r-rcpparmadillo
    - r-rcppprogress
    - if: not (osx and arm64)
      then: libblas
    - if: osx and arm64
      then: libblas * *accelerate
    - if: win
      then: libgomp

tests:
  - script:
      - if: not win
        then: Rscript -e \"library\('RcppPlanc'\)\"
      - if: win
        then: Rscript -e \"library('RcppPlanc')\"

about:
  license: GPL-2.0-or-later
  summary: '''Rcpp'' bindings for ''PLANC'', a highly parallel and extensible NMF/NTF  (Non-negative Matrix/Tensor Factorization) library. Wraps algorithms described in Kannan et. al (2018) <doi:10.1109/TKDE.2017.2767592> and Eswar et. al (2021) <doi:10.1145/3432185>. Implements algorithms described in Welch et al. (2019) <doi:10.1016/j.cell.2019.05.006>, Gao et al. (2021) <doi:10.1038/s41587-021-00867-x>, and Kriebel & Welch (2022) <doi:10.1038/s41467-022-28431-4>.'
  license_file:
    - LICENSE-GPL.md
  homepage: https://github.com/welch-lab/rcppplanc

extra:
  recipe-maintainers:
    - theAeon
    - conda-forge/r

about.summary: 'Rcpp' bindings for 'PLANC', a highly parallel and extensible NMF/NTF  (Non-negative Matrix/Tensor Factorization) library. Wraps algorithms described in Kannan et. al (2018) <doi:10.1109/TKDE.2017.2767592> and Eswar et. al (2021) <doi:10.1145/3432185>. Implements algorithms described in Welch et al. (2019) <doi:10.1016/j.cell.2019.05.006>, Gao et al. (2021) <doi:10.1038/s41587-021-00867-x>, and Kriebel & Welch (2022) <doi:10.1038/s41467-022-28431-4>.
